docs.title: Ronin Server Manager (RSM)
docs.text:
The Ultimate Server Orchestrator

Ronin Server Manager is a high-performance, lightweight management suite designed for gamers who host their own dedicated servers. Built on Electron and powered by PowerShell, RSM provides a professional-grade interface for controlling, monitoring, and maintaining game servers without the overhead of heavy enterprise software.

Download Latest Release Setup Guide

docs.title: 🚀 Key Capabilities
docs.text:
• Unified Process Control

    Forget managing a dozen open windows. RSM tracks your servers using PID (Process ID) Handoff technology. Even if the manager is restarted, it can re-attach to your running servers, ensuring you never lose control of a live environment.

  • Real-Time Resource Monitoring

    Stay ahead of lag. Each server in your list shows live CPU and RAM usage metrics.

    • Monitor Minecraft memory spikes.
    • Track Space Engineers' performance impact in real-time.
    • Visual progress bars help you see exactly how much "breathing room" your hardware has left.

  • Smart Console Integration

    Interact with your servers directly through the built-in console.

    • Monitor startup logs and identify plugin errors instantly.
    • Start and stop all servers at once, or only the one you want.
    • For supported games, RSM captures and displays console output in real-time.
    • Each server has its own console readout for easier troubleshooting.

  • Graceful Shutdowns & Recovery

    RSM handles your data with care.

    • Graceful Exit: For supported games, RSM sends the "stop" command to the console before closing the process, preventing world corruption.
    • Force Kill: If a server hangs or freezes, you can terminate the process tree instantly from the UI.

docs.title: 🛠️ Supported Game Engines
docs.text:
While RSM is a "Universal Launcher," it is specifically optimized for these environments. Select an engine below to see details:

Minecraft Space Engineers SteamCMD & Universal

Full support for Java & Bedrock editions.

  • Memory allocation (-Xmx) support.
  • Automatic .jar execution.
  • EULA Note: Requires one manual run to accept terms.

Minecraft Setup Guide

Optimized for Keen Software House dedicated servers.

  • Console log scraping for real-time monitoring.
  • Specialized tracking for SpaceEngineersDedicated.exe.
  • Automatic process tree attachment.

Space Engineers Guide

Manage any server that runs as a Windows process.

  • SteamCMD: Compatible with Ark, Rust, DayZ, etc.
  • Universal: Supports .bat, .exe, .ps1, and .jar.
  • Console output varies by game engine.

Universal Setup Guide

docs.title: 🔥 The "Internal Fire" Aesthetic
docs.text:
• Customizable Style

    Customizable options for almost every aspect of the manager, so you can make it fit your brand or style.

  • Fast Performance

    Minimal RAM footprint for the manager itself. It stays out of the way of your game's performance.

  • Clean UI

    No cluttered menus—just your servers, your stats, and your console in a high-contrast interface.

docs.title: 📥 Getting Started
docs.text:
1. Download: Grab the latest installer from the Releases page.
  2. Install: Run the .exe (Click More Info -> Run Anyway if Windows warns you).
  3. Configure: Use our Setup Guide to add your first world.

Developed for server admins, by server admins.

docs.title: 🛠️ Troubleshooting & FAQ
docs.text: If your server isn't starting or the console is behaving unexpectedly, check these common solutions.

docs.title: 🔍 Common Issues
docs.text:
• Server Already "Active"

    Issue: RSM shows "Active" but you didn't start the server.

    Fix: RSM uses PID Handoff. It found a running process from a previous session. Click Force Stop if the ID is incorrect.

  • :material-console-off: Blank Console Window

    Issue: The server is running, but no text appears in RSM.

    Fix: * MC: Ensure -nogui is in arguments. * SE: Ensure -console is in arguments. * Admin: Try running RSM as Administrator.

  • :material-ethernet-alert: Port Binding Error

    Issue: "Port 25565 already in use" in logs.

    Fix: A ghost process is holding the port. Kill java.exe in Task Manager or use netstat -ano in PowerShell to find the culprit.

  • :material-account-shield: Permission Denied

    Issue: Server fails to write logs or save world.

    Fix: Ensure RSM has Write access to the server's Root Directory. Running from C:\Program Files\ often requires Admin rights.

docs.title: 🧠 Advanced Technical Info
docs.text:
How PID Handoff Works

Unlike simple batch files, RSM saves the Process ID (PID) to a local database. 1. When you click Start, RSM records the ID. 2. If RSM crashes or is closed, the server keeps running. 3. Upon restart, RSM "scans" for that ID. If it's still alive, it re-attaches the controls instantly.

Memory Leaks

If your RAM usage bars in RSM are hitting 90%+, the manager may struggle to capture logs. Always ensure you leave at least 1-2GB of "Headroom" for the Windows OS to breathe.

docs.title: 📥 Still Need Help?
docs.text:
If your issue isn't listed here, please collect your logs from the appdata/roaming/RSM/logs folder and open a ticket.

Report an Issue (WIP) Join the Community (WIP)

docs.title: 🏗️ Server Setup Overview
docs.text:
Adding a server to Ronin Server Manager is straightforward, but it requires two key pieces of information: the Executable Path and the Arguments.

In some cases (like Minecraft) you may even need a 3rd location for the Working Directory.

Be sure to follow the steps for your specific server in order to get everything working properly. Each game engine has its own quirks when it comes to how it handles console output, process management, and file paths, so read carefully!

docs.title: ⚠️ Prerequisite: The "First Run"
docs.text:
Before adding a server to RSM, ensure the server is fully configured and has been launched at least once manually.

This includes adding any mods, accepting Eula's, and confirming that the server starts without errors. RSM relies on this "first run" to establish the correct file paths and configurations.

Pay attention to any arguments that servers run automatically when starting, and any file paths that they look for in their default state. RSM will need to replicate these conditions to function properly.

  • Run Once Manually

    Double-click your .bat or .exe file to ensure the server starts without errors before linking it to RSM.

  • Add Your Mods*

    If your server uses mods, add them to the server's mod folder or GUI and run the server once to ensure they load correctly.

  • License/EULA

    Ensure you have accepted any EULAs (like Minecraft's eula.txt) or licenses required by the game engine.

  • Firewall Rules

    Ensure you have opened the necessary ports (e.g., 25565 or 27015) in your Windows Firewall settings.

docs.title: 🎮 Select Your Game Engine
docs.text:
Pick your game below for specific pathing requirements and startup arguments.

  • Minecraft (Java)

    Setup for Java Edition. Requires pointing to java.exe and using -nogui.

    View Guide

  • Space Engineers

    Native Windows binary setup. Requires Admin rights for network binding.

    View Guide

  • SteamCMD Games

    Generic setup for Ark, Rust, and other Steam dedicated binaries.

    View Guide

docs.title: 🛠️ Troubleshooting Paths
docs.text:
Server says 'Stopped' immediately

  1. Check Quotes: If your path has spaces (e.g., C:\My Games\), wrap the path in double quotes.
  2. Verify Permissions: Ensure the user running RSM has "Read/Write" access to the game folder.

docs.title: Minecraft (Java Edition)
docs.text: Minecraft runs inside a Java Virtual Machine. RSM needs the direct path to your java.exe to ensure console capture and version stability.

docs.title: ☕ Identifying your Java Path
docs.text:
Manual Method Log Method
  1. Locate Installation: Usually in C:\Program Files\Java\.
  2. Find the Bin: Open your version folder (e.g., jdk-21) and open the bin folder.
  3. Copy Path: Right-click java.exe and select "Copy as path".
  1. Run your server manually once.
  2. Look for a log line like: installed at C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-21\bin\java.exe
  3. Copy that exact path into the RSM Path field.

docs.title: ⚙️ Startup Arguments
docs.text:
To run Minecraft, you point to the Java executable, not the .jar file.

  • Path: Use your java.exe path found in the steps above.
  • Arguments: -Xmx4G -Xms2G -jar server.jar nogui
    • nogui: Required. RSM needs this to capture console output.
    • -Xmx4G: Allocates 4GB of Max RAM.

Pro-Tip: Isolated Java

If you use CurseForge or Prism, they download "Isolated" Java versions. Check their settings for these paths to ensure perfect mod compatibility.

docs.title: Space Engineers
docs.text: Space Engineers runs as a native Windows executable. Unlike Java games, it points directly to the game binary.

docs.title: 🔍 Executable Path
docs.text: Point RSM directly to the dedicated server binary: .../DedicatedServer64/SpaceEngineersDedicated.exe

docs.title: ⚙️ Startup Arguments
docs.text:
These flags are required to ensure RSM can monitor the process correctly.

  • Arguments: -console -ignorelastsession -path "your_instance_folder"
    • -console: Enables the console output for RSM logging.
    • -ignorelastsession: Skips "unclean shutdown" prompts.
    • -path: Defines your custom world/instance storage location.

Admin Rights

Space Engineers often requires Administrative privileges to bind to network ports. Ensure the user running RSM is an Administrator.
